description: dev-rules hooks. All logic in `scripts/dev_rules_hook.py` (executed directly via shebang; stdlib-only, no `uv run`). Subcommands: pre-edit, session-end, session-start. Session-scoped dedup state under `.claude/tmp/dev-rules/injected-<sid>.txt` — one line per announced `<category>:<key>` pointer (e.g. `logging:general`, `logging:python`). Rulesets are organized as `rulesets/<category>/<key>.md`; the dispatcher auto-discovers every category subdirectory at runtime. PreToolUse on Write|Edit|MultiEdit returns `permissionDecision: "deny"` with `permissionDecisionReason` listing **pointer paths** (not bodies) to each category's `general` ruleset (once per session per category) plus the language ruleset matching the target file's extension (once per session per `<category>:<language>`). The model Reads the pointed files and retries the same edit; dedup keys are recorded before the deny is emitted so the retry is not denied again. PreToolUse does not honor `additionalContext`, which is why the deny path is used. SessionEnd deletes this session's state file; SessionStart sweeps orphan files older than 1 day. All hooks exit 0; pre-edit may deny via decision payload but never via non-zero exit.

hooks.description: Deny first matching edit per session per `<category>:<key>` with a reason listing pointer paths to general + language ruleset(s) the model must Read before retrying
Delete this session's dev-rules dedup state
Sweep orphan dev-rules dedup state files older than 1 day
